items.briefing:
With the FIFA World Cup approaching, the FBI is warning about fake websites designed to steal money and personal information from fans. Australians looking to buy tickets or merchandise online should be especially cautious, as these scams can look convincingly legitimate. Separately, Carnival Cruise has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people — if you or a family member have ever booked with them, your personal details may have been exposed.

A recent survey also found Australians are increasingly uneasy about how their data is being used by AI systems, reflecting growing concerns about privacy and consent.

**Tip:** Before purchasing anything related to the FIFA World Cup online, go directly to the official FIFA website rather than clicking links from emails or social media.

featured.title: Australians more concerned about privacy as trust in AI remains low, OAIC survey finds
featured.quote: Australian trust in AI and data privacy concerns directly impact how organisations handle citizen information.
